[ About me ]

Praneeth Suresh

NUS Computer Science + Mathematics student building inspectable AI systems, agentic workflows, and rigorous technical writing.

I care about the layer where research taste becomes working software: model evaluation, representation analysis, reliable agents, static knowledge systems, and the engineering habits that make complex systems easier to inspect.

Static systems, workflows, developer tooling

public repositories reviewed

AI Society

Tech and Research Director of the NUS AI Society.

Public work

Notes, essays, projects, and GitHub repositories that can be inspected directly.

/ Current focus

Turning AI research questions into inspectable systems.

My current research interests are interpretability, representation analysis, model evaluation, memory, routing, agent reliability, and the systems layer around AI research.

My working style is to pair deep reading with implementation: read papers, identify mechanisms, build small systems, and make the tradeoffs visible enough for someone else to inspect.

/ Credibility

Academic depth, leadership, and public proof of work.

Education

NUS Computer Science student with a second major in Mathematics and a perfect GPA.

Leadership

Tech and Research Director of the NUS AI Society, and Tech Director of the residence student committee.

Public work

Maintains a technical notes site, AI research reading trail, GitHub project trail, and selected project case studies.

Curiosity is only useful when it becomes a system someone else can understand, run, and build on.

/ Contact

Contact me about research, internships, consulting, or NUS AI Society collaboration.

Useful messages usually start from a specific overlap: a paper, project, team, startup problem, internship role, workshop, sponsor idea, or AI Society collaboration.

Email is the best first step. Include the context, timeline, and the kind of response that would be useful.

Email me Contact page Collaborate
